title: Medical radiographers
description: Photograph people, landscapes, merchandise, or other subjects. May use lighting equipment to enhance a subject's appearance. May use editing software to produce finished images and prints. Includes commercial and industrial photographers, scientific photographers, and photojournalists. Provide radiation therapy to patients as prescribed by a radiation oncologist according to established practices and standards. Duties may include reviewing prescription and diagnosis; acting as liaison with physician and supportive care personnel; preparing equipment, such as immobilization, treatment, and protection devices; and maintaining records, reports, and files. May assist in dosimetry procedures and tumor localization. Produce ultrasonic recordings of internal organs for use by physicians. Includes vascular technologists. (Based on 6 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Decide which images to include, looking for differences between healthy and pathological areas.
Instruct medical staff or students in magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) procedures or equipment operation and assist with on-the-job training of new employees.
Operate magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) scanners and troubleshoot technical issues related to MRI equipment.
Position patients on cradle with immobilization devices, if needed, and ensure appropriate placement for imaging.
Provide headphones or earplugs to patients to improve comfort during MRI scans and explain procedures to patients and families.
Select appropriate imaging techniques or coils to produce required images and conduct inventories to maintain stock of clinical supplies.
Intravenously inject contrast dyes in accordance with scope of practice and perform screening interviews of patients to identify contraindications.
Write reports or notes to summarize testing procedures for physicians and record, process, and maintain patient data or treatment records.
Schedule patient treatment times and appointments, and conduct quality control checks on laboratory equipment or cameras.
Monitor patients' conditions during treatment and report unusual reactions to physician, ensuring patient comfort and safety.
Provide assistance in dressing or changing seriously ill or injured patients and comfort patients during exams, requesting medication if needed.
Administer radiation to specific body parts using radiation therapy equipment, following established practices and standards.
Maintain records, reports, or files as required, including information such as radiation dosages and equipment settings.
Test and check MRI equipment prior to use to ensure proper functioning and to assist physicians in complex procedures.
Prepare contrast material, radiopharmaceuticals, or anesthetic drugs under the direction of a radiologist, and educate patients on treatment reactions.
Position patients accurately for treatment according to prescription, and check radiation therapy equipment for proper operation.
Review physicians' orders to confirm prescribed exams and determine patients' x-ray needs by reading instructions from physicians.
Calculate treatment dosages delivered during sessions and gather information on patients' illnesses to guide diagnostic procedures.
